acceptance_targets: [chapter_16_f001] In the revised confrontation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:195 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:197, Earl still ends Marcus's place in the group and Marcus visibly absorbs the blow, but the exact phrase "You're not one of us anymore" is absent and the replacement line reads as Earl's terse register rather than copied calibration text.

[chapter_16_f002] In the revised closing scene at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:223 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:277, the reader can identify a live pressure in the room while Harry talks, at least two concrete consequences of the pharmacy switch on actual bodies or care failures, and a Carmen question or reaction that alters the shape of the disclosure. If the passage can be lifted out of the scene and read as a clean background briefing with no loss, it still fails.

[chapter_16_f003] In the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:67, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:113, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:189, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:199, a cold reader can still infer Harry's anger, Agnes's compression, Marcus's self-repair, Earl's authority, and Harry's age or clarity from action, syntax, and image alone. The narrator no longer uses the explicit labels cited above, and no climactic line is immediately followed by a paraphrase whose only job is to tell the reader what the line already did.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised confrontation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:195 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:197, Earl still ends Marcus's place in the group and Marcus visibly absorbs the blow, but the exact phrase "You're not one of us anymore" is absent and the replacement line reads as Earl's terse register rather than copied calibration text.
findings.problem: Earl's expulsion line reproduces a style-bible calibration line verbatim in the chapter's defining confrontation beat. That breaks the do-not-copy rule and makes the scene feel assembled from voice notes instead of freshly spoken on the page.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace Earl's expulsion beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:195 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:197 with new wording that still ejects Marcus from the ring and still hurts him, but does not reuse the calibration sentence from outline/style_bible.json. Keep Earl spare, final, and union-hardened; let the line be original and scene-specific.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised closing scene at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:223 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:277, the reader can identify a live pressure in the room while Harry talks, at least two concrete consequences of the pharmacy switch on actual bodies or care failures, and a Carmen question or reaction that alters the shape of the disclosure. If the passage can be lifted out of the scene and read as a clean background briefing with no loss, it still fails.
findings.problem: After Marcus leaves, the chapter cools into an orderly origin-story debrief. The reveal is necessary, but the current execution shifts from live aftermath into explanatory report: Carmen mostly prompts, Harry summarizes, and the bodily and institutional injuries that should reframe the ring as survival arrive cleaner and safer than the chapter's own pressure has earned.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the aftermath at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:223, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:239, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:247, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:255 so Harry's origin disclosure stays under present-scene pressure instead of reading like movable exposition. Keep the pharmacy-switch reveal and the closing tactical beat, but give Carmen a pressure-bearing response that changes what Harry says next, and replace the most report-like pain summary with a smaller number of concrete bodily and institutional consequences rendered in-scene language.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:67, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:113, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:189, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:199, a cold reader can still infer Harry's anger, Agnes's compression, Marcus's self-repair, Earl's authority, and Harry's age or clarity from action, syntax, and image alone. The narrator no longer uses the explicit labels cited above, and no climactic line is immediately followed by a paraphrase whose only job is to tell the reader what the line already did.
findings.problem: The narration keeps annotating character states and paraphrasing the force of beats the scene has already made legible. Labels such as "the accountant's version," "Her stress mode," "ledger mode now," and "the self-repair had kicked in," plus repeated not-X/not-Y verdict scaffolds, make the chapter feel explained instead of fully trusted. The prose is strongest when it stays with the concrete room; it weakens when it translates its own effects into commentary.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the interpretive overlays at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:67, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:113, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:179, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:189, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:199, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:227, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:271 so behavior, silence, and physical detail carry the meaning without diagnostic labels. Cut the repeated explanatory verdicts after lines already land, and vary away from the recurring not-X/not-Y sentence scaffold in climactic moments.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
